dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses volume confirmation principle: increase confidence (enter) when price changes with above-average volume (vol_ratio_20), decrease confidence (exit) when volume is light. Direction determined by price movement direction (up/down). 'Relatively heavy volume' interpreted as volume > 20-period ratio baseline. Exit condition inverted from entry logic per Kaufman's principle.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses volume confirmation principle: increase confidence (enter) when price changes with above-average volume (vol_ratio_20), decrease confidence (exit) when volume is light. Direction determined by price movement direction (up/down). 'Relatively heavy volume' interpreted as volume > 20-period ratio baseline. Exit condition inverted from entry logic per Kaufman's principle.

source_rule_text: Increase confidence in price direction changes when they occur on relatively heavy volume; decrease confidence when price changes occur on very light volume.
